rules.statement: Complete deterministic policy preflight before using tools that can modify files or external state.
When policy inputs, resolution, pack, lock, or bootstrap validation is missing or stale, remain read-only until the failure is resolved.
Preserve user-authored global and repository instructions; update only an unambiguous LLM Wiki managed block or managed hook entry.
Run every required check declared in the resolved policy and report its command and exit code.
Do not describe policy text loading, acknowledgement, or self-reporting as proof that an AI understood or followed the policy.
Treat machine-specific dependency, permission, encoding, network, and tool failures as local environment issues; do not promote their workarounds into repository workflows.
Obtain explicit user approval before changing public install or update commands, build configuration, versioning, or supported workflows.

Determine whether software comes from a published package, editable checkout, or another source before giving install or update instructions; never replace a local editable fork with a remote package unless the user explicitly requests it.
